content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2229
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 9, 2008


William A. Vaughn
525 J Street, Suite A
Los Banos, CA 93635


Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-08-100

Dear Mr. Vaughn:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Los Banos City Councilmember Michael Villalta regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Because you do not seek advice regarding a specific governmental decision, we are providing you only informal assistance. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)

Additionally, we base our advice solely on the provisions of the Act and do not address the applicability, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest, Government Code Sections 1090 and 1099, or your agency's incompatible activities policy.

QUESTION

May Councilmember Villalta participate in decisions regarding a proposed annexation to the City of Los Banos that includes the Los Banos Cemetery Board District?

_______________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. 1-08-100
Page No. 2


CONCLUSION

Based on the facts you have provided, Councilmember Villalta does not appear to
have a conflict of interest in participating in governmental decisions regarding the
proposed annexation to the City of Los Banos because the stipend he receives from the
Los Banos Cemetery District Board falls under the Act's "government salary exception."

FACTS

You are the city attorney for Los Banos and you are requesting advice on behalf
of Los Banos Councilmember Villalta. Prior to assuming office, Councilmember Villalta
was an appointed member of the Los Banos Cemetery District Board ("board"). He was
appointed to the board by the Merced County Board of Supervisors in 2006. He was
reappointed in 2007 to an additional four-year term, so he continues to serve as a member
of the board.

As a member of the board, Councilmember Villalta receives a stipend from the
County of Merced in the amount of $100 per meeting. In addition, he receives
reimbursement for any expenses he incurs as a board member.

The Los Banos city council is currently contemplating a proposed annexation to
the city. The annexation includes 466 acres and involves approximately 40 property
owners. Among these property owners is the Los Banos Cemetery District ("district"),
which owns property and manages a cemetery within the area proposed for annexation.
If the annexation is approved, the district will be located within the city of Los Banos.

Councilmember Villalta has no other financial interests other than his position
with the board that is related to, or potentially affected by, the proposed annexation
decision.

You seek advice regarding whether Councilmember Villalta may participate in
decisions involving the annexation of the district.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a
governmental decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the governmental decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the
public official's economic interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) The
Commission has adopted an eight-step standard analysis for deciding whether an
individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a given governmental decision.

File No. I-08-100
Page No. 3

(Regulation 18700(b)(1)-(8).)

Step One: Is Councilmember Villalta a public official for purposes of the
Act?

Under the Act, a "public official" includes "every member, officer, employee or
consultant of a state or local government agency." (Section 82048.) As a member of the
Los Banos City Council, Mr. Villalta is a public official.

Step Two: Will Councilmember Villalta be making, participating in, or
influencing a governmental decision?

A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting
within the purview of his or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Regulation 18702.1.)

In addition, a public official "participates in making" a governmental decision
when he or she, without substantive review, negotiates, advises, or makes
recommendations regarding a decision. (Regulation 18702.2.) Councilmember Villalta
will be participating in making a governmental decision if he advises, recommends or
negotiates a course of action for the city.

Furthermore, a public official is "influencing a governmental decision" if he or
she contacts, or appears before, or otherwise attempts to influence any member, officer,
employee, or consultant of the government agency regarding the decisions. (Regulation
18702.3) Therefore, Councilmember Villalta would be influencing a governmental
decision if he contacts, appears before, or otherwise attempt to influence any member,
officer, employee, or consultant of the city of Los Banos regarding the proposed
annexation.

Step Three: What Are Councilmember Villalta's Economic Interests?

A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or
her immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic interests. (Section
87103; Regulations 18703-18703.5.) The applicable economic interests include:

• An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or
indirect investment of $ 2,000 or more (Section 87103(a); Regulation 18703.1(a)),
or in which he or she is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any
position of management (Section 87103(d); Regulation 18703.1(b));

• An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $ 2,000 or more (Section 87103(b); Regulation 18703.2);

File No. I-08-100
Page No. 4

• An economic interest in any source of income, including promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3);

• An economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $390 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18703.4);

In addition, a public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances (expenses, income, assets, or liabilities), as well as those of his or her immediate family. This is also known as the "personal financial effects" rule. (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5.)

Economic Interests Identified

Source of Income: Your question focuses on potential conflicts arising from Councilmember Villalta's position as a district board member who receives a stipend of $100 per meeting. As noted above, a public official has an economic interest in his or her own income. (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5) and in the source of that income. (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18703.3.)

However, the Act's definition of income expressly excludes "salary and reimbursement for expenses and per diem received from state, local or federal government agency.…" (Section 82030(b)(2), copy enclosed.) Because the district is a local government agency under Section 82041,² the stipend paid to Councilmember Villalta is not considered "income" under Section 82030(b)(2).

Thus, under the "governmental salary" exception, Councilmember Villalta does not have an economic interest in the district as a source of income.

Personal Finances: Every public official has an economic interest in his or her own finances and those of his or her immediate family. (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5.) A governmental decision will have an effect on this economic interest if the decision will result in an increase or decrease in the official's or the official's immediate family members' personal expenses, income, assets or liabilities. (Ibid.)

You have not provided any facts indicating that the proposed annexation to the City of Los Banos would affect Councilmember Villalta's personal finances in any way, therefore, we do not further analyze his personal finances as a potential conflict of interest. However, please note that a conflict of interest may arise if it reasonably

² Section 82041 defines a "local government agency" as "a county, city or district of any kind including school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing."

File No. I-08-100
Page No. 5

foreseeable that the decisions will have an effect on the councilmember's personal
finances.

We conclude that based on the facts you have provided, it does not appear that
Councilmember Villalta has any economic interest that would present a conflict of
interest for him in participating in decisions regarding the proposed annexation to the
City of Los Banos.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5560.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

ER:jgl

Enclosures
